title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Adwick Fenn and Chief Inspector Craddock discuss the potential poisoning involving Marina Gregg and Heather Bardwell. Fenn suggests that multiple individuals could have been involved in the act, but he refuses to speculate on specific suspects.

Detective Superintendent Cumberland, Detective Dermot Klatzko, and Detective Constable Craddock attempt to contact photographer Margaret Bens. After failing to reach her by phone, they visit her studio in a dead-end alley off Tottenham Court Road. Inside, they meet Johnny Jethro, a thin young man who assists Margaret with her scheduling. Jethro explains that the studio phone was unplugged to prevent distractions. Craddock informs Jethro that they are investigating a murder in St. Mary Mead, near Marchbournem, involving poison in a cocktail, and that Margaret was an eyewitness. Jethro offers to drive the detectives to Margaret's current location at the Kitts' house in Hampstead Heath.

Upon arriving at the residence, the detectives observe Margaret directing a photo shoot involving two girls, Jane and Elsie, in an attempt to capture natural poses. When Craddock introduces himself, Margaret—described as thin and skeletal with thick black hair—acknowledges him. Craddock explains he wishes to ask questions regarding the incident at Gossington Hall near Marchbarn, where Margaret was reportedly taking photographs. Margaret confirms she remembers the event well but asks if the detective is Constable Coney.
